subject: create builder for dmaap consumer
commit_message:
create builder for dmaap consumer

Builder in this case is for invoking initProperites method after constructor. Current implementation invokes overrided methods in the constructor, what is wrong.

Change-Id: I63842ea60f318627ae32e734467d2bce602d72e4
Issue-ID: SO-466
Signed-off-by: Lukasz Muszkieta <lukasz.muszkieta@nokia.com>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I'm not sure I understand the purpose of these changes.

Patch Set 3:

Curent implementation invokes overridden methods in constructor DmaapClient class, what is a bad practice. I moved this methods to initProperties method. Builder is made to create object, and then invoke initProperties method.

Patch Set 3:

> Curent implementation invokes overridden methods in constructor
 > DmaapClient class, what is a bad practice. I moved this methods to
 > initProperties method. Builder is made to create object, and then
 > invoke initProperties method.

This is an abstract class and this issue does not apply to this case. I agree it is strange but it does not warrant a solution such as this.

Patch Set 3:

> > Curent implementation invokes overridden methods in constructor
 > > DmaapClient class, what is a bad practice. I moved this methods
 > to
 > > initProperties method. Builder is made to create object, and then
 > > invoke initProperties method.
 > 
 > This is an abstract class and this issue does not apply to this
 > case. I agree it is strange but it does not warrant a solution such
 > as this.

It doesn't matter if class is abstract or not. Constructor should never call non-static methods, especially abstract ones (and even calling static ones is bad), because the object is not fully initialized. This can easly lead to dangerous and unpredictable situations, like accessing member that's initialized in non-abstract class.

Patch Set 3:

> > > Curent implementation invokes overridden methods in constructor
 > > > DmaapClient class, what is a bad practice. I moved this methods
 > > to
 > > > initProperties method. Builder is made to create object, and
 > then
 > > > invoke initProperties method.
 > >
 > > This is an abstract class and this issue does not apply to this
 > > case. I agree it is strange but it does not warrant a solution
 > such
 > > as this.
 > 
 > It doesn't matter if class is abstract or not. Constructor should
 > never call non-static methods, especially abstract ones (and even
 > calling static ones is bad), because the object is not fully
 > initialized. This can easly lead to dangerous and unpredictable
 > situations, like accessing member that's initialized in
 > non-abstract class.

Please show me how to instantiate and instance of an abstract class and we can continue with the point you're trying to make. I agree with you that it's not the most accepted way to solve this particular problem, but we have little risk here. If you want to talk about risk, it seems mighty easy with your implementation for the caller to forget to initialize the required properties for the DmaapConsumer and DmaapPublisher. If only there was a way to guarantee that our objects completed all their required initialization before being used....wait a minute.

Patch Set 3:

Programming is about trade offs. I find developers are more likely to forget to call some "init" method themselves than incorrectly create an implementation of an abstract class.

Patch Set 3:

> Programming is about trade offs. I find developers are more likely
 > to forget to call some "init" method themselves than incorrectly
 > create an implementation of an abstract class.

Ok, to be clear, I know the init method is not the best option (however is far away better than calling abstract methods in constructor), this the first step for better refactoring. 
Anyway, I set constructors private, and init method is protected and invoked from build method of builder. Look more carefully, the builder is responsible for initialization of whole necessary fields when object is created.

Patch Set 3:

Seshu, could you please give your opinion?
Abandoned
